sutta: and this is another quite important sutra the buddha said monks there are these 5 spheres of release wherein the unreleased mind of among abiding zealous arden and resolute finds release all the ah not yet wholly destroyed come to complete destruction all the unsurpassed peace from effort not yet attained is 1 what 5 monks take the case when the master or some fellow in the holy life who acts as teacher instructs among in dhamma while he teaches that monk but partakes of both the spirit and the letter of this dharma from this experience joy pamoja springs up and from that delight pity in such a state his whole being calms down when he is calm he's sukkah is experienced and for him who dwells at ease the mind is concentrated monks this is the first sphere of release wherein the unreleased mind of among abiding zealous arden and resolute finds release all the asavas not yet wholly destroyed come to complete destruction all the unsurpassed peace from effort not yet attained is 1 again monks when the master or some fellow in the holy life does not instruct him in dhamma but he himself instructs others in detail as he has heard it as he has learned it by heart what he does teach us he partakes of this dhamma and experiences joy delight calm and ease wherein his mind is concentrated monks this is the second sphere of release or else he repeats dhamma as he has heard it as he has learned it and while doing so he partakes of this dhamma and experiences joy delight harm and ease whereby his mind is concentrated monks is the third sphere of release or else in his heart he ponders and reflects on dhamma as he has heard it as he has learned it he reviews it carefully in his mind and while doing so he partakes of this dhamma and experiences joy delight calm and ease whereby his mind is concentrated monks this is a fourth sphere of release or else some concentration sign samadhi nimita is rightly grasped by him rightly held by the attention rightly reflected on rightly penetrated by wisdom and while this takes place he partakes of both the spirit and letter of this dharma from this experience joy springs up from that delight in such a state his whole being calms down when he is calm ease is experienced and for him who dwells at east the mind is composed monks this is the fifth sphere of release wherein the unreleased mind of among abiding zealous ardent and residue finds release all the asavas not yet wholly destroyed come to complete destruction or the unsurpassed peace from effort not yet attained is 1 monks these are the 5 spheres of release it's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is a very important suta because here you find the buddha is trying to stay here the different conditions where a person a monk gets released you know or the asaphas are destroyed that means he becomes an arahant and what are these 5 conditions first 1 is hearing dhamma hearing the teacher teach dharma and when he is listening to the dhamma he partakes of the spirit and letter of this dharma and from this joy arises and then delight and then because of these 2 he is calm and when he becomes calm the mind becomes concentrated when the mind becomes concentrated and that is the condition for wisdom to arise and he gets released so the first 1 is hearing dhamma that is why hearing dharma the sutras is very important we pay attention and hear dhamma carefully put all the attention there it is possible as in many sutas the buddha shown that a person can enter the stream the second 1 is a person teaches dhamma and when he teaches dharma because when he teaches dhamma he has to reflect on the dhamma wonder in his mind as to how to explain it and then he partakes of the dhamma and joy delight calm and ease arises and his mind becomes concentrated and he attains release that is the second condition the third 1 he repeats dhamma repeating dhamma is also very useful that means you learn the dhamma and instead of just going in 1 ear and going out the other ear you listen to it again and again or if you have the words of it you repeat it especially where certain sutas are very important you keep on repeating it and you understand more and more and so that is the third way you get released and you get attainment of arya hood the fourth 1 ponders and reflects on dhamma this is also very useful to ponder and reflect on dhamma that you have heard perhaps tonight you hear the dhamma tomorrow when you when you're more free yeah you can think about the dhamma that you have heard now reflect on it and perhaps you understand that in that was said and then you can attain release then the fifth 1 is to contemplate this concentration sign the sign of meditation and when you constant when you when you contemplate on the concentration sign then the mind automatically becomes concentrated and from there the concentrated minor wisdom arises and you attain uh release so these are the 5 uh ways now 1 thing you would like to note now is that the condition for calming down the mind here is that joy and delight arises joy and delight arises that means that you've got to have joy and uh delight not not all dukha you know meditation is not all dukkha it is all dukkha joy and delight cannot arise uh that is why in the sutras the buddha talked about the bliss of seclusion the bliss of renunciation referring to pity and sukkah which from where concentration arises so remember that meditation is not sitting down and seeing a lot of dukkha is too much dukkha then you have no inspiration to meditate can't keep it up so you got to have this inspiration from the joy and the delight of meditation and from there you be calm down and attain concentration and after that wisdom so these 5 important these are the 5 ways of attaining arya hood remember again first 1 is listening to dhamma second 1 teaching dhamma you can also teach dharma to somebody else if you have a listen to it often enough from the tapes or from the books third 1 is repeating dhamma repeat the passage of dhamma again and again the fourth 1 is to ponder and reflect on dhamma the fifth 1 is contemplating the concentration sign so if you want to attain to aryahudna now the nexuta is
